examples: CMC does recommend using a Halyard Clamp for easy installation.
At almost the same time our Genoa halyard jammed and we couldn't get the sail down.
The halyard typically rises from the head of the sail to a block at the head of the mast and back down to deck level, from where it is pulled up to raise the sail.
But suddenly we heard a loud pop as a halyard--the line used to hoist the front sail--snapped.
It was then briefly unable to hoist its spinnaker pole until a new halyard could be fixed, and Victory's lead extended considerably.
